description: Read fuel sensor data transmitted by GPS hardware, detect significant fuel drops (theft or fast consumption) and unexpected increases (refuelling), and provide fuel consumption summaries across trips and time periods.

actors.name: GPS Device
actors.description: Reads fuel sensor and includes fuel level in position transmissions

actors.name: Position Processing Pipeline
actors.description: Compares fuel levels between consecutive positions to detect threshold crossings

actors.name: Fleet Manager
actors.description: Monitors fuel levels, reviews consumption reports, and investigates anomalous drops

fields.name: fuel_level
fields.description: Current fuel level as a percentage (0–100%) from the sensor

fields.name: fuel_volume
fields.description: Current fuel volume in litres (device-specific unit)

fields.name: fuel_used
fields.description: Cumulative fuel consumed since device reset or reference point

fields.name: fuel_consumption_rate
fields.description: Instantaneous fuel consumption rate (litres per hour or per 100 km depending on device)

fields.name: fuel_drop_threshold
fields.description: Minimum decrease in fuel level/volume that triggers a fuel drop event

fields.name: fuel_increase_threshold
fields.description: Minimum increase in fuel level/volume that triggers a fuel increase (refuel) event

rules: Fuel data is read from position attributes; the specific attribute key and unit depend on the device and protocol
A fuel drop event is generated when the fuel level decreases by more than fuel_drop_threshold between consecutive positions
A fuel increase event is generated when the fuel level increases by more than fuel_increase_threshold between consecutive positions (indicating a refuel)
Only latest positions are evaluated for threshold crossings; outdated positions are skipped
Fuel consumption is calculated per trip and per summary period using the difference between start and end fuel values
If the device does not report a fuel sensor, the feature is inactive and no fuel events are generated
Both percentage-based and volume-based fuel attributes are supported; the platform uses whichever the device provides

given: current fuel value is lower than previous fuel value by >= fuel_drop_threshold
position is the latest for the device

then.description: Fuel drop event recorded with type = device_fuel_drop, previous and current values

fuel_drop_detected.result: Fuel drop event stored; possible theft or leak investigation can be triggered

given: current fuel value is higher than previous fuel value by >= fuel_increase_threshold
position is the latest for the device

then.description: Fuel increase event recorded with type = device_fuel_increase, previous and current values

fuel_increase_detected.result: Fuel increase event stored; refuelling noted for cost tracking

given: position does not include any fuel attribute
no_fuel_sensor.result: Fuel events skipped for this position; no fuel data is available from this device

errors.message: The device referenced does not exist

events.name: fuel.dropped
events.description: Fuel level fell by more than the configured threshold — possible theft, leak, or high consumption

events.name: fuel.increased
events.description: Fuel level rose by more than the configured threshold — refuelling detected

related.description: Fuel values arrive as position attributes from device transmissions
Trip reports include fuel consumption from start to end of each trip
Fuel summary reports aggregate consumption over configurable periods
